factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in frac sand, intermodal, petroleum products, and automotive parts shipments.

evidence.text: Volume grew 2%, with increased demand in shale-related frac sand, intermodal, petroleum products, and automotive parts shipments, which more than offset declines in grain, coal, and rock shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key areas, indicating positive momentum.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, supported by higher fuel surcharge revenue and core pricing gains.

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 7% during the first quarter of 2018 compared to 2017 resulting from higher fuel surcharge revenue, core pricing gains, and 2% volume growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices with clear evidence of revenue growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by higher pricing and volume growth.

evidence.text: Freight revenues increased 7% during the first quarter of 2018 compared to 2017 resulting from higher fuel surcharge revenue, core pricing gains, and 2% volume growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating organic growth driven by pricing and volume.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, with a 2% increase in carloadings across several commodity groups.

evidence.text: Volume grew 2%, with increased demand in shale-related frac sand, intermodal, petroleum products, and automotive parts shipments, which more than offset declines in grain, coal, and rock shipments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, supporting positive trends.

factors.summary: The energy and premium segments are growing fastest, while agricultural and industrial segments show mixed performance.

evidence.text: Energy – Freight revenue from energy shipments increased 15% in the first quarter of 2018 compared to 2017 due to 6% volume growth, mix of traffic, and higher fuel surcharge revenue. Premium – Freight revenue from premium shipments increased 7% in the first quarter compared to 2017 due to higher fuel surcharge revenue, core pricing gains, and 2% volume growth, more than offsetting negative mix of traffic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in key segments despite mixed performance elsewhere.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly fuel prices, which increased by 28% year-over-year.

evidence.text: Fuel  – Fuel includes locomotive fuel and fuel for highway and non-highway vehicles and heavy equipment. Locomotive diesel fuel prices, which averaged $2.13 per gallon (including taxes and transportation costs) in the first quarter of 2018, compared to $1.75 per gallon in the same period in 2017, increased expenses by $102 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant rise in fuel prices leading to higher expenses.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with a slight increase of 1% year-over-year, partially offset by reductions in management and administrative costs.

evidence.text: Compensation and Benefits – Compensation and benefits include wages, payroll taxes, health and welfare costs, pension costs, other postretirement benefits, and incentive costs. For the first quarter, expenses increased 1% compared to 2017 due to volume-related costs, network operational challenges, and increased Train, Engine and Yard (TE&Y) training expenses. Lower management and administrative wage and benefit costs partially offset the increases.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Slight increase offset by reductions, showing mixed impact.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving, with the operating ratio improving by 0.6 points year-over-year.

evidence.text: Operating Ratio – Operating ratio is our operating expenses reflected as a percentage of operating revenue. Our first quarter operating ratio of 64.6% improved 0.6 points mainly driven by core pricing gains and volume growth, which more than offset higher fuel prices and other cost hurdles.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are delivering measurable savings, with $35 million in savings net of additional expenses.

evidence.text: In the first quarter of 2018, our productivity initiatives yielded approximately $35 million, net of additional expenses due to network operational challenges, including higher labor, equipment rental, locomotive maintenance, and fuel consumption costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings of $35 million indicate cost improvements despite additional expenses.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, with progress in resource productivity initiatives, including engineering capital projects and workforce reductions.

evidence.text: Despite our operational challenges, we handled volume growth of 2% with a 1% decline in workforce levels, demonstrating progress in other resource productivity initiatives, including engineering capital projects along with management and administrative reductions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in operational efficiency and successful workforce reductions support productivity gains.

factors.summary: The company is in contraction mode, with a 1% decrease in average employees year-over-year, driven by workforce reduction plans.

evidence.text: Employee levels decreased 1% in the first quarter of 2018 compared to the same period in 2017 despite a 2% increase in volume levels. A smaller capital workforce and fewer management and administrative personnel drove the reduction and more than offset an increase in TE&Y employees in training.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: 1% decrease in workforce indicates contraction and potential negative impact on employment.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, with a focus on productivity initiatives and efficiency improvements.

evidence.text: Despite our operational challenges, we handled volume growth of 2% with a 1% decline in workforce levels, demonstrating progress in other resource productivity initiatives, including engineering capital projects along with management and administrative reductions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on productivity and efficiency improvements reflects management's positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include network congestion, higher fuel prices, and inflationary pressures on purchased services.

evidence.text: Network congestion on key routes and terminals, created in part by high freight car inventory levels, negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell. Continued implementation and testing of Positive Train Control across a larger portion of our network also negatively impacted overall average train speed. Operating expenses increased $192 million in the first quarter compared to 2017 driven by higher fuel prices, volume-related costs, network operational challenges, depreciation, and increased state and local taxes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks like network congestion and higher fuel prices pose challenges to performance.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and network congestion are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Higher fuel surcharge revenue resulted from higher year-over-year fuel prices, partially offset by a lag in fuel surcharge recovery due to the sequential increase in fuel price from the fourth quarter of 2017 (it can generally take up to two months for changing fuel prices to affect fuel surcharge recoveries). Network congestion on key routes and terminals, created in part by high freight car inventory levels, negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fuel prices and network congestion are significant sensitivities impacting results negatively.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is challenged by network congestion and higher terminal dwell times.

evidence.text: Network congestion on key routes and terminals, created in part by high freight car inventory levels, negatively impacted overall fluidity, average train speed, and terminal dwell. Average terminal dwell time in the first quarter of 2018 increased 8% compared to the same period of 2017 largely due to network congestion on key routes and terminals, created, in part, by high freight car inventory levels and execution challenges.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Network congestion and higher terminal dwell times negatively impact supply chain efficiency.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing, with adjusted debt to capital rising from 43.9% to 45.1%.

evidence.text: Adjusted debt to capital is a non-GAAP financial measure under SEC Regulation G and Item 10 of SEC Regulation S-K, and may not be defined and calculated by other companies in the same manner. We believe this measure is important to management and investors in evaluating the total amount of leverage in our capital structure, including off-balance sheet lease obligations, which we generally incur in connection with financing the acquisition of locomotives and freight cars and certain facilities. Adjusted debt to capital should be considered in addition to, rather than as a substitute for, debt to capital. The tables above provide reconciliations from debt to capital to adjusted debt to capital.
evidence.subsection: Debt to Capital / Adjusted Debt to Capital

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Increase in debt leverage is a factual measure without clear positive or negative implications.
